source_jobs.title: Acquisitions Manager, DAC region (Contract)
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their communities and build a lasting business, including paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live experiences, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately, our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free new memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ fans paying each month for exclusive access to creators' work and community.

We’re continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the strongest team in the creator economy. As part of our goal to expand into Europe, we’re looking for an Acquisitions Manager to help drive creator growth, strengthen our regional presence in the DAC Region, and support our mission to empower creators to build sustainable businesses on Patreon.

This role is based in London.

About the Team

The Creator Acquisition team at Patreon is focused on helping the world’s digital creators launch sustainable businesses through membership and direct-to-fan community-building. We work directly with creators and their teams to launch and grow on Patreon, offering personalised support, proven best practice strategies, and the tools they need to succeed. Our mission is simple: to empower creators to own their work, deepen connections with their communities, and thrive creatively and financially on Patreon.

About the Role

 - Own and drive a robust pipeline of creators, enabling them to launch their memberships on Patreon

 - Leverage your personal network to find points of contact and forge relationships with key players in the creator economy

 - Execute on sequenced acquisition email campaigns on a daily basis

 - Lead sales calls with digital creators with subject-matter expertise in launching and growing a subscription business

 - Manage pipeline via CRM reporting & tracking

 - Stay informed & communicate to the team on latest emerging trends in the creator economy with proposed acquisition targets and actionable leads

About You

 - 4-5 years of experience working in the creator space, specifically working with creators on monetisation strategies

 - Experience collaborating with Legal, Data Science, Marketing, and Finance teams and using data-backed strategies to inform strategic decision-making

 - Deep passion for the creator economy, podcasting & the intersection of social video platforms, and creators in general

 - Take pride in putting creators first

 - Highly motivated, energised by project management, and excels in a fast paced environment working alongside a growing team

 - Extraordinary organisational skills, multitasking skills, and remain detail-oriented at all times

 - Preferred qualifications: experience working with top tier podcast and video creators on YouTube

 - Native or bilingual proficiency in German preferred; full professional proficiency minimum

About Patreon

Patreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:

 - Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re here. When creators win, we win.

 - Build with Craft | We sign our name to every deliverable, just like the creators we serve.

 - Make it Happen | We don’t quit. We learn and deliver.

 - Win Together | We grow as individuals. We win as a team.

We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage

source_jobs.title: Creator Growth Strategist (Contract)
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Creator Growth Strategist (Contract) to support our mission.



This role is remote on a ~20 hour/week contract basis.




ABOUT THE TEAM

Creator Growth exists to help Patreon's creators build sustainable, growing businesses on the platform. We work across scaled programming and 1:1 strategic support, meeting creators where they are — from their first fan to their biggest year yet.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a part-time Creator Growth Strategist to help scale a 1:1 growth consults program supporting small and medium sized creators. You'll run one-time strategic growth consults, helping creators walk away with clear, tactical next steps for growing their business on Patreon. For ~20 hours a week, you'll own follow-up to make sure that advice gets acted on, run a recurring outreach cadence to keep the pipeline full, and help pilot new formats for 1:1 support as the program scales.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Experience in a creator-facing, customer success, or SMB advising role, or equivalent experience

 - A track record of advising creators or small businesses on growth, with measurable results

 - The ability to quickly understand a creator’s business and offer tangible, tactical recommendations on the spot

 - Strong attention to detail and follow-through; you'll be tracking consults, outreach, and follow-up across a growing pipeline

 - Strong presence on video calls and a customer-success mentality; you treat every creator's growth as your own win

 - Comfort owning a high-volume calendar directly (including renegotiating conflicts) while keeping each interaction feeling personal, not transactional

 - Comfort operating in an ambiguous, evolving program and adapting to new formats as they're piloted

 - Fluency with CRM/outreach tools (Salesforce, Airtable, or equivalent)
   
   


BONUS IF YOU HAVE

 - Experience at a creator platform, MCN, or creator-focused startup

 - Experience advising SMB or small business owners on growth

We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.




ABOUT PATREON

Patreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:

 - Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re here. When creators win, we win.

 - Build with Craft | We sign our name to every deliverable, just like the creators we serve.

 - Make it Happen | We don’t quit. We learn and deliver.

 - Win Together | We grow as individuals. We win as a team.

Patreon is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran stat

source_jobs.title: Growth Partner Manager, EMEA (Contract)
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their communities and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live experiences, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

 

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 60 million+ free new memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 10 million+ fans paying each month for exclusive access to creators' work and community.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Growth Partner Manager, EMEA (contract) to support our mission.

This role is based in London and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.

 

About the Team

The Top Creator Management team oversees and drives platform satisfaction and growth for the highest impact and highest potential creators on Patreon. You’ll report to the Head of Top Creator Management.

 

About the Role

This fast paced and high-impact role requires a great level of complex problem-solving with both internal and external key partners. You’ll be responsible for the account management & success of a dynamic book of business partners and driving data-backed solutions and opportunities.

 - Drive strategy and support for a high-volume dynamic portfolio of creators, offering timely support, clear guidance, and knowledge sharing of best practices.

 - Lead strategy sessions with creators to grow their communities, increase revenue, or better use Patreon’s tools, both in a 1:1 and 1:many included capacity.

 - Respond to inbound questions with speed and clarity, helping creators feel heard and supported

 - Share updates and learning resources with creators while keeping communication personal and actionable

 - Identify and escalate common issues, feedback, or trends to improve products and processes

 - Champion partner feedback to product teams for optimization of internal and external tools.

 - Collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to continuously improve the creator experience
   Keep systems and documentation up to date to support smooth handoffs and knowledge sharing amongst the team

 - Manage and negotiate complex deal structures, partnering closely with finance and legal teams.

 

About You

 - BA/BS degree or higher

 - 4+ years in customer success, partnerships, or partner/account management, accountable for the success of existing customers, long-term ongoing 1:1 relationships, and revenue retention/growth in a B2B2C or B2B SaaS environment.

 - 2+ years in the creator space influencing creator success, ideally with podcasts, video, or music creators.

 - Expertise in using data and analytics to develop insights for decision making.

 - Excellent communication and presentation skills; must share and pitch ideas to partners and potential partners

 - Analyze creator cohort performance and engagement through data-driven research, diagnostics, and benchmarking.

 - Experience influencing customer behavior to drive success through effective change-management practices, coaching, and training

 - Curious, empathetic, and confident offering strategic advice in short, focused interactions

 - Track record of revenue and product‑adoption growth within a medium- to large- strategic portfolio.

 - Patient, creative, adaptable, strategic, data-driven, collaborative, and tech + AI savvy.

 - High operational rigor - disciplined CRM usage, cadence execution, and follow‑through.

 - Strong negotiation/persuasion skills - both strategic and tactical. Experience building complex deals is a plus.



Growth & Learning Opportunities

 - Understand the complex business models

source_jobs.title: Staff Product Designer, Discovery
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.



We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Staff Product Designer to support our mission.



This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

We're hiring a Staff Product Designer to join our Discovery and Community team and help define what Patreon becomes next.

Your work will center on how fans find creators and communities that are meaningful to them, shaping how creator work is surfaced across a platform built for connection over attention, and how new content formats and experiences deepen those relationships over time.

You'll be part of a small, close-knit design team, partnering closely with Product, Engineering, and Research to bring clarity to ambiguous problems and ship experiences with polish and purpose.




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Proactively discover company-level opportunities and help bring clarity and insight through high-fidelity prototypes and expert storytelling

 - Work closely with Research, Product, and Engineering to identify, plan, scope, and execute end-to-end design solutions

 - Work across the entire design organization to develop innovative patterns and systems that are durable, repeatable and achieve a high standard for craft

 - Create, collaborate, and iterate on prototypes, high-fidelity visuals, and key flows

 - Contribute to the visioning, sequencing, tactics, and execution of a multi-phased overhaul of the product

 - Own, redefine, and ship key parts of the core Patreon product




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 8–12 years of product design experience or equivalent depth demonstrated through the complexity, scope, and systems-level thinking evident in your portfolio

 - A portfolio of shipped work demonstrating ownership from idea to outcome, with measurable impact and clear articulation of decisions and tradeoffs

 - Leads multiple concurrent work streams and sets clear milestones with cross-functional partners

 - Experience facilitating problem-framing and alignment across product, engineering, and research teams using a broad toolkit including frameworks, prototypes, research synthesis, and workshops

 - A craft champion who sets a high quality bar and elevates the standard for teams around them

 - Takes a team-first approach to decisions, inspires cross-functional partners toward a unified design vision, and operates as a true strategic peer across the organization



We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.



About Patreon

Patreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:

 - Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re here. When creators win, we win.

 - Build with Craft | We sign

source_jobs.title: Senior Engineering Manager, Platform
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Senior Engineering Manager o support our mission.



This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

We're looking for a Senior Engineering Manager to lead the teams responsible for the core infrastructure and platform services that power Patreon's product and ecosystem.

This role is ideal for a senior engineering leader who has:

 - Scaled a consumer, UGC, or marketplace platform to millions of users

 - Led infrastructure and/or platform teams supporting product engineering at scale

 - Operated in environments with high availability, bursty traffic, and complex distributed systems

You'll partner closely with Product, Security, Data, and Product Engineering leaders to ensure Patreon's platform is reliable and scalable.

 
 


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Lead 2–3 Platform & Infrastructure teams encompassing backend services, databases, and reliability foundations.

 - Own roadmap and execution strategy for your teams, aligned to org-wide and business goals.

 - Drive platform initiatives across core backend services, service reliability, performance and scalability.

 - Partner closely with Product, Security, Data, and peer engineering leaders.

 - Develop and mentor engineering managers directly reporting to you.

 - Contribute to area-wide multi-year strategy alongside the Director.

 - Produce written and verbal updates to the Director and cross-functional partners on risks, tradeoffs, and platform investment decisions.

 
 


ABOUT YOU

 - 10+ years of engineering experience, including 5+ years in engineering management, or equivalent demonstrated leadership experience

 - Experience leading platform, infrastructure, or foundational backend teams

 - Demonstrated experience scaling a consumer product, UGC platform, or marketplace to millions of users

 - Experience designing and operating distributed systems, APIs, and service-oriented architectures in a production environment

 - Experience operating production systems with high availability and performance requirements

Bonus if you have

 - Background in developer platforms, internal tooling, or platform abstractions

 - Familiarity with cloud infrastructure (AWS/GCP), data pipelines, and event-driven systems

 - Experience leading through major architectural transitions or scale inflection points



About Patreon

Patreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:

source_jobs.title: Scaled Program Manager (Contract)
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Scaled Program Manager (Contract) to support our mission.

This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Scaled Creator team is part of Creator Success and exists for one reason: to help every small and medium sized creator on Patreon feel supported, and grow their business on Patreon. We're a small team managing over 300,000 creators, so everything we do is high touch at scale. We work across everything from scaled communications to 1:1 gold glove relationship management, meeting every creator where they are in their journey.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for a Scaled Program Manager (Contract) to own the programming layer for the top of the Mid-Market creator portfolio. This is a new role built for someone who wants to design a playbook and series of programs from scratch.

You'll build and run scaled, higher-touch programs for top mid-market creators, serving as a touchpoint for ~600 creators. You'll design email series, run office hours, publish newsletters, and build 1:many programming touchpoints that feel personal at scale. And when a creator in your cohort needs more (i.e. a growth conversation, a product walkthrough, a real human in their corner) you'll know when to step in.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 5 or more years of experience in creator success, partnerships, community management, or a related creator-economy role, or equivalent experience

 - Demonstrated experience building or running scaled programs, lifecycle email campaigns, or creator programming

 - Written communication skills demonstrated at scale

 - Fluency with CRM and outreach tools (Salesforce, Airtable, or equivalent) and the ability to use data signals to prioritize work across a large creator cohort

 - A track record of building repeatable processes and playbooks

 - Able to define your own structure in an early-stage role - this program doesn't exist yet, and you'll be building the approach from the ground up


BONUS IF YOU HAVE

 - Active or former creator yourself

 - Experience at a creator platform, MCN, talent management firm, or creator-focused startup



We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you're excited about a role but your past experience doesn't match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you're a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we'd love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Senior Machine Learning Engineer, Relevance
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Senior Machine Learning Engineer to support our mission.

 

This role is based in San Francisco or New York as an in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.

 


ABOUT THE TEAM

You'll join the Relevance team, whose mission is to build the ML systems that power how fans discover creators and how content surfaces across Patreon. The team is responsible for search, ranking, feed relevance, and creator-fan matching. You'll work closely with a small, collaborative group of MLEs on shared infrastructure, code reviews, and roadmap alignment, while partnering cross-functionally with Product, Data Engineering, and Trust & Safety to deliver measurable impact across the platform.

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Conduct exploratory data analyses and proof-of-concept machine learning models to understand opportunities and potential project impact.

 - Collaborate with cross-functional partners, such as product, engineering, design, legal, and trust and safety to design effective machine learning solutions.

 - Analyze and prepare training data, including using crowdsourcing data labeling techniques.

 - Train and iterate on machine learning models using novel techniques.

 - Deploy machine learning models to production and write backend code when necessary to properly deploy the model.

 - Debug models when observability shows performance gaps, and iterate on models.

 


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Experience working in an end-to-end machine learning team environment (typically 5+ years): analyzing data, building and iterating on machine learning models, writing production-level code and shipping to production, monitoring performance, and A/B testing.

 - Writes clean and robust code in Python or other programming languages, and provides substantive, constructive feedback in code reviews.

 - Experience debugging complex systems with a systematic approach.

 - Analyzes datasets thoroughly to develop product and customer insights.

 - Writes clear technical documentation for both technical and non-technical audiences.

 - Seeks and incorporates feedback on code, models, and technical approaches.

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field, or the equivalent
   
   

We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.

source_jobs.title: Director of Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is the creative network where 300,000+ creators and millions of fans discover, connect, and invest in each other. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business, including paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and one-time purchases, along with a feed to discover—and get discovered by—people who share their interests.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ in revenue generated since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best discovery platform with the best team in the creator economy, and are looking for a Director of Product Marketing to support our mission.

This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.



About the Team

MarComms (Marketing & Communications) at Patreon is a scrappy and mighty team made up of our Brand Marketing, Product Marketing, Lifecycle Marketing, Creative, PR, Events, Social, Creator Partnerships, Media, Video, and Program Management teams. Our key cross-functional partners include Product, Acquisitions, and Creator Success teams.The Director of Product Marketing role reports to the SVP, Marketing.



About the Role

Patreon is building the defining platform for the creator economy and we're looking for a Director of Product Marketing to shape how we tell that story to the world.

As Director of Product Marketing, you'll be the connective tissue between our product and the creators and fans we serve. You'll define how Patreon positions its product in the market, translate complex features into compelling value propositions, and lead the go-to-market strategy for everything we ship. This is a role for someone who thinks in systems: you see how positioning, messaging, and launch execution compound over time into a brand that creators trust and fans love.

Reporting to the SVP of Marketing, you'll partner closely with Brand, Creative, Social, Comms, and Creator-facing teams to ensure that Patreon's product investments land with the creators and fans they were built for. You'll bring the voice of the market into product decisions and the voice of product into the market. And you'll build and lead the team that makes both happen at scale.



What you'll do

 - Own Patreon's product positioning and messaging framework. This includes defining who we're for, what makes us different, and how to articulate our value at every stage of the creator and fan journey

 - Lead go-to-market strategy for major product launches, coordinating cross-functional execution from narrative development to adoption measurement

 - Build and maintain deep market intelligence: understand creator and fan motivations, competitive alternatives, category trends, and unmet needs that inform both product and marketing strategies

 - Partner with Product leadership to influence roadmap prioritization by surfacing insights that sharpen our bets and reveal emerging opportunities

 - Develop the tools, frameworks, and narratives that enable Creator Success, Partnerships, and Acquisitions teams to pitch Patreon's value with conviction

 - Define how we measure product marketing impact — from feature adoption to creator activation — and build the feedback loop that connects market outcomes back to product and leadership

 - Recruit, develop, and lead a high-performing Product Marketing team



About You

 - 10+ years of experience in product marketing, with at least 3 years in a senior or lead role owning positioning and go-to-market strategy for a consumer or creator-facing product

 - Proven track record of leading high-impact product launches end-to-end from defining the narrative to driving measurable adoption and retention outcomes

source_jobs.title: Porto Site Lead
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Porto Site Lead to support our mission.

This role is based in Porto, Portugal and operates on a hybrid work model (in-office 2 days per week).




ABOUT THE TEAM

Trust & Safety keeps Patreon welcoming and fair so Creators and their Fans can connect and creativity can thrive. We set clear, easy-to-follow standards and act quickly and consistently when something's off, giving creators and fans confidence to create, share, and participate with respect across our global community.

Our Porto site is our safety heartbeat in Europe: most of our frontline Trust & Safety work happens here. It's a real community where people support each other, share context, and carry tough work together. Porto is also a source of honest insight — feedback from the site has shaped policy decisions, tooling asks, and how we scale with people, BPOs, and automation. As Patreon becomes more public and discovery-led, Porto's experience is central to how we evolve.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a Porto Site Lead in our Porto location to keep the safety pipeline healthy during EU hours and make sure work moves seamlessly between Porto and the U.S. You'll be Porto's advocate for safety operations — turning local signals into global action, helping teams adopt improvements that stick, and explaining what changed and why in plain language.

You'll sit in Trust & Safety so you have real operational context, but your remit is site-wide: chairing the Porto Leadership Council, partnering with the Admin/HR partner, and helping represent the needs of all Porto teams (not just T&S) in big decisions. You'll lead through influence at first (no direct reports initially, with dotted-line relationships) and report to the Director of Trust & Safety Operations (based in the U.S.).

As the site and product evolve, there is room for this role to grow into a broader people-lead scope based on business need and alignment with our leveling framework.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Experience in Trust & Safety, risk, operations, or a related field, including site or regional leadership responsibility — typically 7–10 years, or equivalent combination of experience and scope

 - Experience leading teams or sites in the EU/UK region

 - Has measurably improved operational performance in a prior role — able to point to specific changes made and outcomes achieved using data and structured problem solving

 - Experience working with or managing vendors/BPOs

 - Communicates clearly in English and Portuguese — able to translate complex safety decisions into plain language for local teams and explain operational changes to a U.S.-based leadership team

 - Comfort operating in a global, matrixed environment and leading through influence as well as direct management

 - Familiarity with EU/UK online safety expectations and how they translate into day-to-day operations




BONUS IF YOU HAVE

 - Experience representing a regional site in global or cross-functional planning processes

 - Understands what a healthy online community looks like and can connect day-to-day safety operations to that outcome



We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is crit

ABOUT THE TEAM

The Payments Experience team is establishing Patreon's position as the most trusted direct-to-creator payments platform for fans and creators through seamless, delightful payments experiences. This team owns payments problems end-to-end on both web and mobile, from the purchase experience for fans all the way through the account balance withdrawal experience for creators, and the systems and settings in between. You'll have the opportunity to shape the future of how creators get paid, and you'll work closely with experienced designers, engineers, and go-to-market teams to deliver thoughtful, high-quality product experiences.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

We ship often, sweat the details, and aren't afraid of an ambitious roadmap.

We're looking for a fullstack engineer to help develop the systems and APIs that will power future user-facing features. In any given week you might be asked to:

 - Eagerness to take on new problems and grow your technical, collaboration, and leadership skills.

About the Team

The Creator Acquisition team at Patreon is focused on helping the world’s digital creators launch sustainable businesses through membership and direct-to-fan community-building. source_jobs.description:

We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Product Designer to support our mission.



This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

We're hiring a Product Designer to join Creator Success, shaping the core experiences that help creators build sustainable businesses and engaged communities on Patreon. You'll design tools and touchpoints that help creators publish their work, attract and retain fans, and present their creative offerings in compelling ways. Your work will span how creators showcase their content, onboard to the platform, and grow their audiences, ensuring both free and paid experiences feel polished and purposeful.

You'll tackle high-impact problems across the creator journey. You'll design publishing workflows for podcasters, video creators, and newsletter writers. You'll shape how creators present their work to potential fans. And you'll build systems that help creators understand what to do next to succeed. This is a collaborative role where you'll partner closely with Product, Engineering, and Research to bring clarity to ambiguous opportunities and ship experiences that directly influence how hundreds of thousands of creators grow their businesses on Patreon.




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - Create, collaborate, and iterate on prototypes, high-fidelity visuals, and key flows

 - Contribute to the visioning, sequencing, tactics, and execution of a multi-phased overhaul of the product

 - Own, redefine, and ship key parts of the core Patreon product




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 7+ years of end-to-end product design experience, or equivalent demonstrated through your portfolio

 - Portfolio showing design work from problem framing to shipped product, with clear evidence of business or user impact

 - Demonstrated ability to manage multiple workstreams across stakeholders without losing detail or pace

 - Experience applying design thinking methodologies to lead teams toward user-centered outcomes

 - Holds a high bar for visual and interaction quality — able to articulate why a design decision is right, not just that it feels right

 - Moves quickly from insight to execution, comfortable working with incomplete information

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Scientist, Feed Relevance
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their communities and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live experiences, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases.

Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $8 billion+ in revenue generated since Patreon's inception

 - 60 million+ free new memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 10 million+ fans paying each month for exclusive access to creators' work and community.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Data Scientist to support our mission.

This role is based in New York or San Francisco and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Relevance team owns feed ranking and recommendations across Patreon's core surfaces — Home Feed, Membership Feed, Post Page Recommendations, and Niches. We're building the systems that decide what content fans see and in what order, with the goal of making every fan's experience on Patreon feel personally relevant. Our cross-functional pod includes ML Engineers, Product Managers, Backend Engineers, Client Engineers, and Data Scientists working together to improve content discovery, deepen fan engagement, and drive creator monetization. We're at an exciting inflection point: our ranking and recommendation systems are being built largely from scratch, which means you'll have outsized influence on the technical direction and measurement frameworks that shape how millions of fans discover creators.




ABOUT THE ROLE

 - You'll own the analytics foundation for Patreon's feed — building and maintaining the trustworthy metrics infrastructure that powers dashboards, experiment analysis, ML training data, and leadership reporting.

 - You'll design and analyze ranking experiments across Home Feed, Membership Feed, Post Page Recs, and Niches, working closely with ML Engineers to evaluate relevance changes and new ranking signals.

 - You'll conduct exploratory analyses to surface data-backed hypotheses about feed health, content mix, engagement patterns, and discovery conversion — and translate those into actionable ranking improvements.

 - You'll develop feed health and engagement dashboards with drill-downs by surface, user segment, platform (iOS vs. Android), and feed version.

 - You'll be a thought partner to Product and ML Engineering, helping the team prioritize ranking experiments and measure what matters — from scroll depth and CTR to Discovery Attributed TPV.

 - You'll contribute to the technical and collaborative direction of the data science team.




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - Experience working on a content feed, ranking system, or recommendation platform — including familiarity with retrieval, scoring, and tradeoffs involved in optimizing for multiple objectives.

 - Deep expertise in A/B testing on ranking or recommendation changes, with a strong statistical inference foundation.

 - Experience building metrics and reporting infrastructure that teams rely on as a source of truth — dashboards, data quality pipelines, and alerting.

 - Strong SQL proficiency and experience using Python or R for statistical analysis.

 - Experience navigating shifting priorities and systems.

 - Experience making tradeoffs between speed and accuracy when analytical direction is unclear.

 - Experience working closely with product, ML engineering, and data engineering partners — including translating between technical findings and business priorities.

 - Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Economics, Engineering, Mathematics, a related quantitative field, or equivalent practical experience.

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Storage
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is a media and community platform where over 300,000 creators give their biggest fans access to exclusive work and experiences. We offer creators a variety of ways to engage with their fans and build a lasting business including: paid memberships, free memberships, community chats, live video, and selling to fans directly with one-time purchases. Ultimately our goal is simple: fund the creative class. And we're leaders in that space, with:

 - $10 billion+ generated by creators since Patreon's inception

 - 100 million+ free memberships for fans who may not be ready to pay just yet, and

 - 25 million+ paid memberships on Patreon today.

We're continuing to invest heavily in building the best creator platform with the best team in the creator economy and are looking for a Senior Storage Platform Software Engineer to support our mission.

This role is based in San Francisco or in New York and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

The Storage Platform team is the foundation Patreon is built on. We design and operate the core data systems and the services that tie them together — that power every creator page, membership transaction, and fan interaction on the platform.


Our mission is to build the data foundation that lets Patreon scale without limits. Every creator page load, membership payment, and fan interaction depends on systems we design and operate. As Patreon grows to support hundreds of thousands of creators and tens of millions of members, we ensure the platform stays fast, resilient, and dependable under massive scale.


We tackle some of the most challenging infrastructure problems at the company — distributed systems, data consistency, scalability, reliability, and developer productivity while giving engineers deep ownership over architecture and technical direction. Our work enables every engineering team at Patreon to move faster and build with confidence.


If you care deeply about systems correctness, performance at scale, and building infrastructure that other engineers love building on top of — this is your team.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Your responsibilities will include:

 - Designing and implementing backend systems that handle millions of requests per day

 - Writing efficient, scalable, and maintainable code

 - Identifying and addressing performance bottlenecks

 - Building our robust APIs that support our web and mobile applications

 - Collaborating with other engineers, product managers, and designers to deliver high-quality features on time




WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 8+ years of experience in backend software engineering

 - Proficiency in Python or a similar language

 - Strong understanding of software design patterns and principles

 - Experience with relational databases (such as PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (such as Redis)

 - Experience with container orchestration (such as Kubernetes)

 - Able to communicate clearly about technical decisions for both technical and non-technical audiences

 - Experienced working across engineering and product teams to scope and ship features

 - Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field — or equivalent professional experience



We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.

 - Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re h

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer, Backend Platform
source_jobs.description:
Patreon is the best place for creators to build memberships by providing exclusive access to their work and a deeper connection with their communities. We’re building a content and community platform where creators can engage directly with their fans and monetize their creativity, while maintaining full ownership over the work they make and the communities they create.

We’re leaders in the membership space with 250,000+ active creators and over $3.5 billion paid directly to creators on our platform. Our team is building tools to optimize the creator-to-fan relationship, including native video, enhanced podcasting features, improved creation tools, and new community experiences. We’re continuing to invest heavily in building the most talented team in the Creator Economy and are looking for a Software Engineer to support our mission.

This role is based in SF and open to those who are able to be in-office 2 days per week on a hybrid work model.




ABOUT THE TEAM

As a Staff Backend Platform Software Engineer at Patreon, you will be responsible for designing and implementing scalable, high-performance backend systems that power our creator membership platform. You will collaborate with engineers and product managers to build and maintain reliable, efficient systems that support Patreon's growth.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Your responsibilities will include:

 - Designing and implementing backend systems that handle millions of requests per day

 - Writing efficient, scalable, and maintainable code

 - Identifying and addressing performance bottlenecks

 - Building robust APIs that support our web and mobile applications

 - Collaborating with engineers, product managers, and designers to deliver high-quality features on time
   
   


WHAT YOU'LL NEED

 - 8+ years of experience in backend software engineering, or equivalent demonstrated experience

 - Strong experience with Python or a similar language

 - Strong understanding of software design patterns and principles

 - Experience with relational databases (such as PostgreSQL) and NoSQL databases (such as Redis)

 - Experience with container orchestration (such as Kubernetes)

 - Strong understanding of RESTful API design and development

 - Writes clearly for technical and non-technical audiences, and collaborates effectively across engineering and product teams to scope and ship features

 - Familiarity with continuous integration and deployment (CI/CD) practices

 - Bachelor's, master's, or doctoral degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or a related field, or the equivalent
   
   

We hire talented and passionate people from different backgrounds because workplace diversity and inclusion is critical to our ability to serve creators worldwide. If you’re excited about a role but your past experience doesn’t match with every bullet point outlined above, we strongly encourage you to apply anyway. If you’re a creator at heart, are energized by our mission, and share our company values, we’d love to hear from you.



About Patreon

Patreon powers creators to do what they love and get paid by the people who love what they do. Our team is passionate about making this mission and our core values come to life every day in our work. Through this work, our Patronauts:

 - Put Creators First | They’re the reason we’re here. When creators win, we win.

 - Build with Craft | We sign our name to every deliverable, just like the creators we serve.

 - Make it Happen | We don’t quit. We learn and deliver.

 - Win Together | We grow as individuals. We win as a team.



Patreon is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We provide employment opportunities without regard to age, race, color, ancestry, national origin, religion, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, veteran status, or any other protected class.

Patreon offers a competitive benefits package including and not limited to salary, equity plans, healthcare, unlimited paid time off, company hol

notes: Patreon has a mix of tech and non-tech roles, with a strong focus on engineering and product development.
